notes: No per-set published study for Crimson Invasion (SM4), so SM-era averages are applied -> confidence "low". Era baselines come from Flipside Gaming "A Comprehensive Review of Rarity Part Two" (re-fetched and confirmed): regular holo GX ~1/9 packs; Ultra Rare (full-art GX + full-art Trainer) ~1/18-36 packs, draft uses midpoint ~1/24; Secret Rare (rainbow + gold) ~1/2 booster boxes ~= 1/72 packs. Coarse-bucket mapping was VERIFIED against the live pokemontcg.io sm4 rarity counts (re-fetched via curl since WebFetch 403'd; counts match the draft EXACTLY): Common 34 -> common; Uncommon 26 -> uncommon; Rare 20 + Rare Holo 13 = 33 -> rare; Rare Holo GX 9 + Rare Ultra 11 = 20 -> engine ultra-rare(19); Rare Rainbow 8 + Rare Secret 5 = 13 -> engine secret-rare(13, EXACT). Engine ultra-rare bucket = regular holo GX + full-art GX + full-art Trainers, so per-pack rates were SUMMED: ultra-rare = P(GX 1/9) + P(full-art 1/24) = 0.111111 + 0.041667 = 0.152778. secret-rare (rainbow+gold) = 1/72 = 0.013889. rare (holo + non-holo) = remainder = 0.833333. Each slot sums to exactly 1.0 (re-validated arithmetically). Box-level cross-check: 0.152778*36 = 5.5 GX-class hits/box and 0.013889*36 = 0.5 secret/box (the "1 in 2 boxes" expectation) -> one rare-or-better hit per pack, sane, no over-claiming. Pack structure = standard SM: 4 commons, 3 uncommons, 1 reverse-holo (modeled as 50/50 common/uncommon filler, no value), 1 rare-or-better EV slot; 10th card is a basic Energy and is not modeled. Distribution keys are a strict subset of the set's available buckets {common, uncommon, rare, ultra-rare, secret-rare}; this set has no shiny-rare (no Shiny Vault). This is a STANDARD SM set, not a special set: 36-pack Booster Display and 8-pack ETB both confirmed. Only change vs draft: swapped the two un-fetchable ToyWiz product URLs for the canonical Pokemon Center product pages (36-pack Booster Display and ETB), whose pack counts I independently confirmed across Pokemon Center, Amazon, Walmart, Pokemon.com, TCGplayer, and Pristine Auction.
